retrieval_text: Bayberry is a medicinal herb profile in the Herbalisti historical corpus. It is represented across 188 works and 765 source-linked chunks. Recorded variants include Bayberry, Bay-berries, Bay- Berry, Bayberries, Bay-berrys. Common plant-part co-mentions include Bark, Root, Berry, Leaf, Seed, Gum. Common preparation co-mentions include Tea, Powder, Tincture, Oil, Emetic, Decoction. Common condition co-mentions include Cold or catarrh, Wounds or ulcers, Vomiting or nausea, Dysentery, Diarrhoea or looseness, Fever. Caution co-mentions include Dose caution, Child-use caution, Bleeding caution, Narcotic or sedative caution, Allergy or sensitivity caution, Toxicity watch. Representative source works include The practice of medicine on Thomsonian principles : adapted as well to the use of families as to that of the practitioner : containing propositions illustrative of the philosophy of Thomsonism : a brief history of the symptoms, peculiarities, and general course of disease in its different forms and varieties : with practical directions for administering a Thomsonian course of medicine, including the various methods of administering vapour baths and emetics, and a materia medica , adapted to the work; The practice of medicine on Thomsonian principles : and a materia medica, adapted to the work / by J. W. Comfort, M.D.; The American vegetable practice, or a new and improved guide to health, designed for the use of families ... / By Morris Mattson.; The American vegetable practice : or a new and improved guide to health, designed for the use of families : in six parts. This is a retrieval summary of historical co-mentions only, not a medical claim.
